titleStmt.title: Letter from B. N. Mahaul, 28 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: B. N. Mahaul

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from B. N. Mahaul. Mahaul is writing in the hope that the letter will be brought to the attention of the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland Sir Ivor Churchill Guest, Lord Wimborne (1873-1939). Mahaul was writing to complain about the newspaper 'The Eye-Opener'. The paper caused some controversy due to an article it published with the heading 'Black Peril' which attacked Indian students. The paper claims that 'coloured people' were savage and that any white person seen in their company was a disgrace. Mahaul asks the lord lieutenant to act against the offensive paper.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5575/5

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
28th March,1916 Dear Sir, May I ask you to oblige
me by bringing the following
to the notice of His Excellency
the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland. The editor of the paper
eye-opener is doing a great
mischief in inciting race
against race by his articles
'Black Devil'. He has been
writing against very many
people here. But his allowance
to Condemn a race under the
British rule are surely doing
more harm than is visible
at present. Individuals whom he
has been libeling might not
have thought fit to bring
an action against him.
But maker becomes ruinous
when he attacks a class
of His Majesty' subjects. During the war such a
wretched publication can
easily be stopped under
the Defence of the Realm
Act for inciting race
hatred and disturbing
peace. I may add that my
information is that the Editor
is not a responsible person
and has been in jail
before. The doings ofâ
such a man should
surely be met with
strong measures.
By so doing His Excellency
will be placing a sector
of the loyal subjects of His
Majesty under a deep
debt of gratitude. Hoping my request
shall move His Excellency
who is known to be so sympathetic
and popular over here
Yours most obedient servant. B. N. Mahaul.
